description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing nasal congestion, sneezing, and mild discomfort.
recommendedActions: Rest and drink plenty of fluids
Use saline nasal spray for congestion
Gargle warm salt water for sore throat
whenToSeekCare: If fever exceeds 103°F, symptoms last more than 10 days, or breathing becomes difficult

description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses, more severe than the common cold.
recommendedActions: Rest at home and avoid contact with others
Drink plenty of fluids to stay hydrated
Take antiviral medication if prescribed early
Use fever reducers like ibuprofen or acetaminophen
whenToSeekCare: If you have difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain, confusion, or severe vomiting

description: A respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus with a wide range of symptoms.
recommendedActions: Monitor oxygen levels with a pulse oximeter
whenToSeekCare: Emergency: difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain, confusion, inability to stay awake, bluish lips

description: An infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs which may fill with fluid.
recommendedActions: Rest and drink plenty of fluids
Use a humidifier to ease breathing
whenToSeekCare: Immediately if you have difficulty breathing, chest pain, or high persistent fever

description: A neurological condition causing intense debilitating headaches often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light.
recommendedActions: Rest in a dark quiet room
Apply cold or warm compress to head
whenToSeekCare: If headache is the worst of your life, comes on suddenly, or is accompanied by fever and stiff neck

description: High blood pressure that can lead to serious health complications if left untreated.
recommendedActions: Exercise regularly and maintain healthy weight
whenToSeekCare: If blood pressure reads above 180/120 or you have chest pain, vision problems, or difficulty speaking

description: A chronic condition affecting how the body processes blood sugar leading to high glucose levels.
recommendedActions: Follow a low sugar balanced diet
whenToSeekCare: If you experience confusion, rapid breathing, fruity breath odor, or loss of consciousness

description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines typically caused by viral or bacterial infection.
recommendedActions: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions
Eat bland foods like rice toast and bananas
Avoid dairy fatty and spicy foods
Rest and avoid contact with others
whenToSeekCare: If vomiting or diarrhea lasts more than 2 days, signs of dehydration, blood in stool, or high fever

description: An infection in any part of the urinary system including kidneys bladder and urethra.
recommendedActions: Drink plenty of water to flush bacteria
Use heating pad for pain relief
whenToSeekCare: If you have high fever, back pain, nausea, or symptoms do not improve after 2 days of antibiotics

description: A condition in which airways narrow swell and may produce extra mucus making breathing difficult.
recommendedActions: Avoid known triggers like dust and smoke
whenToSeekCare: If rescue inhaler does not help, breathing is severely difficult, or lips turn blue

description: An immune system response to a foreign substance that is not typically harmful to most people.
recommendedActions: Apply hydrocortisone cream for skin reactions
whenToSeekCare: Emergency: swelling of throat, difficulty breathing, dizziness or drop in blood pressure

description: Inflammation of the appendix causing severe abdominal pain requiring immediate medical attention.
recommendedActions: Do not eat or drink anything
Do not take pain medications before diagnosis
Do not apply heat to abdomen
whenToSeekCare: IMMEDIATELY — this is a medical emergency requiring surgery

description: A condition where you lack enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to body tissues.
recommendedActions: Eat iron-rich foods like spinach and red meat
Take vitamin C to improve iron absorption
Get regular blood tests to monitor levels
whenToSeekCare: If you experience chest pain, severe shortness of breath, or rapid heartbeat

description: A digestive disease where stomach acid or bile irritates the food pipe lining.
recommendedActions: Avoid spicy fatty and acidic foods
Do not lie down immediately after eating
whenToSeekCare: If you have difficulty swallowing, persistent vomiting, or unintentional weight loss

description: A mental health disorder characterized by persistent excessive worry about daily situations.
recommendedActions: Practice deep breathing and relaxation techniques
whenToSeekCare: If anxiety interferes with daily life, relationships, or you have thoughts of self-harm

description: A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness loss and disinterest in daily activities.
whenToSeekCare: Immediately if you have thoughts of self-harm or suicide — call emergency services

description: Hard deposits made of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys causing severe pain.
recommendedActions: Drink plenty of water to help pass stone
Strain urine to catch the stone for analysis
whenToSeekCare: If pain is unbearable, accompanied by fever, chills, or blood in urine

description: A life-threatening disease caused by parasites transmitted through infected mosquito bites.
recommendedActions: Rest and drink plenty of fluids
whenToSeekCare: Immediately — malaria can be life-threatening especially if symptoms worsen rapidly

description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi spread through contaminated food and water.
whenToSeekCare: Immediately if fever is very high, severe abdominal pain, or confusion occurs

description: A mosquito-borne viral disease causing flu-like illness and occasionally severe potentially lethal complications.
whenToSeekCare: Immediately if bleeding from nose or gums, blood in vomit, severe abdominal pain, or rapid breathing

description: Inflammation of the sinuses often caused by infection allergies or autoimmune issues.
whenToSeekCare: If symptoms last more than 10 days, fever is high, or vision changes occur

description: Inflammation of one or more joints causing pain and stiffness that worsens with age.
recommendedActions: Apply hot or cold packs to joints
whenToSeekCare: If joints become very swollen, you have fever with joint pain, or cannot perform daily activities

description: A medical emergency where blood flow to the heart is blocked causing heart muscle damage.
recommendedActions: Do not drive yourself to hospital
whenToSeekCare: CALL EMERGENCY SERVICES IMMEDIATELY — this is a life-threatening emergency

description: A medical emergency where blood supply to part of the brain is cut off causing brain damage.
recommendedActions: Do not give food or water
Keep person calm and lying down
whenToSeekCare: CALL EMERGENCY SERVICES IMMEDIATELY — every second counts with stroke

description: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy blister-like rash on the skin.
recommendedActions: Keep fingernails short to prevent scratching
whenToSeekCare: If rash spreads to eyes, difficulty breathing, confusion, or rash becomes very red and warm

description: A serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
recommendedActions: See a doctor immediately for testing
Take full course of prescribed antibiotics
whenToSeekCare: Immediately — TB requires proper diagnosis and long-term treatment
